ideas.title: Blocking pre-commit `cargo build --locked` + test gate for the daemon & apply_concept
ideas.why: The needs-review flag and the bfada18 revert of the 20-commit email-gate prove the autonomous loop ships non-compiling code (caught only afterward), so a shift-left Deterministic-verification-of-generated-code gate must run BEFORE any commit reaches main — the recurring root-cause signal in logs/needs-review.md.

ideas.title: 3D WebGL force-directed knowledge graph for the 200-concept ontology / /kb
ideas.why: The Knowledge-graph-viz and WebGL-brain-HUD concepts plus the newly shipped 136-page KB give the code the substance to justify a navigable 3D graph (borrowing the whoa-factor of Obsidian's graph view) — a memorable, shareable showpiece that makes /kb a destination instead of a flat index.

ideas.title: Live compute-topology visualizer: spark-1/spark-2 GPUs + tiered-router spark-vs-cloud split
ideas.why: The HN signal 'Distributed / mesh LLM compute' and the Tiered-LLM-router + Decentralized-mesh-inference concepts map directly onto the current-signals telemetry (two reachable NVIDIA GB10 nodes, router spark:68 vs cloud_hard:1345) — visualize where each inference actually runs to make the mesh real and observable.

ideas.title: Agent-provenance / egress-audit panel: click any pipeline run and inspect exactly what each agent sent, received & changed
ideas.why: Grounded in today's HN signal 'Auditing what AI agents actually send' and the Agent-egress-transparency + Data-minimization concepts — turn the existing knowledge graph into a queryable, click-to-inspect provenance graph that proves what the dark factory's own agents transmit, the single most on-trend build for this repo right now.

ideas.title: Honor prefers-reduced-motion & forced-colors on the factory HUD animations
ideas.why: The Forced-colors-mode concept flags a real accessibility gap — the animated factory/vitals HUD has no reduced-motion or high-contrast fallback, an a11y fix that is pure CSS (no Rust logic, can't break the build) and makes the site usable for motion-sensitive and high-contrast users.

ideas.title: Write an essay: 'What our own agents send upstream' — egress transparency, from first principles
ideas.why: Trend-grounded-content-synthesis on the day's strongest world signal (auditing agent egress) plus the Learn-from-scratch HN theme — a truthful, grounded post about this factory's real data-minimization posture is content the audience is actively searching for and only this system can write authentically.
